description_long: This is a small, classic biplane with a striking blue and white radial pattern on its wings and fuselage, accented by gold and silver details. The aircraft features two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, with the upper wing slightly larger than the lower. The nose houses a single propeller engine, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, all fixed and exposed. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, both decorated with the same blue and white motif. The cockpit is located just behind the upper wing, appearing to be a single-seat, open or semi-enclosed design. The overall appearance is reminiscent of aerobatic or sport biplanes, with a lightweight, agile build and visually dynamic livery.
description: A small, blue and white biplane with a radial pattern, single propeller, and fixed landing gear. It features a conventional tail and a single-seat cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and symmetrical blue and white radial pattern.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the fuselage's streamlined shape, cockpit placement, and the fixed main landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail's conventional design, the blue and white pattern on the tail surfaces, and the alignment of the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the propeller, cockpit, and the continuation of the radial pattern along the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, appearing semi-enclosed.
